source_jobs.title: Principal Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Heron processes millions of sensitive financial documents for some of the fastest-growing MCA Funders, Brokers, Insurers, and Fintechs in the US and UK. We're at a point where the volume, velocity, and complexity of the data flowing through our systems and is outgrowing the way they were originally built. We need someone who's done this before: taken a platform that works and made it scale.




THE CHALLENGE

We've built a document automation and workflow orchestration platform that lenders, funders and fintechs rely on to make real decisions. It's working, customers love it, and we're onboarding new ones faster than ever.

The challenge is that our backend was built for speed-to-market, and now we need to make it built for scale. Concretely, that means:

 - Abstractions that let us move fast: The backend systems and patterns that let a 15-person engineering team ship product confidently without breaking things for existing customers.

 - Multi-tenancy at scale: Dozens of enterprise customers sharing the same cloud infrastructure, each with different data sensitivity requirements, SLAs, and integration patterns.

 - Data volume and velocity: High-throughput processing of financial documents (bank statements, tax returns, pay stubs) with ML pipelines that need to be fast, accurate, and reliable.

 - Security and compliance: Sensitive financial data handled to exacting standards.

We’re excited about this challenge because it is the kind of problem that shows up when things are going well! We're hiring this role because we couldn’t be more confident in our trajectory but need to build the foundations for the next 10x.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

This is a hands-on, high-autonomy IC role. You'll work directly with our CTO (Dom) and Head of Engineering (Oli), and you'll have real ownership over the systems that matter most.

In your first few months, you'll likely:

 - Assess our current backend architecture and identify the highest-leverage areas for improvement around reliability, scalability, and security.

 - Partner with the platform and infrastructure team to design and implement the core abstractions (data model, data pipeline reliability, deployment patterns) that will support our next phase of growth.

 - Set the technical direction for how we scale our infrastructure from handling hundreds of customers to thousands, and from millions of documents to tens of millions.

 - Raise the bar on backend engineering practices across the team: not through process for process's sake, but by building systems that make the right thing the easy thing.

Longer-term, this role shapes how Heron's entire platform evolves. You'll be the person who ensures our backend can support whatever product throws at it.




WHO THRIVES HERE

The best person for this role has likely spent 8+ years building and scaling backend systems, with at least a couple of meaningful "at-bats" taking a system from early stage to serious scale. You've probably worked in B2B SaaS and have opinions (earned the hard way) about how to build systems that are both reliable and fast to develop on.

More specifically:

 - You have a strong CS foundation and are deeply technical. You write code, review architectures, and debug production issues.

 - You've seen and built backend systems scale at companies growing from Series A to Series C+ (roughly $5M to $100M+ ARR). You know what breaks and when.

 - You have recent production experience with Python and cloud infrastructure.

 - You've dealt with sensitive data at volume: financial, health, or similarly regulated domains.

 - You're an IC at heart. You might set direction and mentor others, but you want to build, not manage.

 - You care about getting things right but also about shipping. We're a startup. Pragmatism matters.


WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT AS A HERON

 - Competitive compensation, including equity

 - Based full-time in London or NYC, where most of the team is in-person multiple days per week

 - Your choic

source_jobs.title: Product Manager (London)
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT MANAGER

We're hiring a Product Manager to help us build the future of automated document workflows at Heron. You'll own a product vertical end-to-end - from customer discovery to shipping features that eliminate hours of manual work for our customers every day. If you love talking to customers, shipping fast, and working at the intersection of AI and financial services, we'd love to hear from you.




THE OPPORTUNITY

Every day, thousands of small businesses apply for funding. A broker emails over a stack of bank statements, tax returns, and application forms - sometimes scanned, screenshotted or photographed on someone's phone. A team of underwriters then spends hours doing the same manual review, over and over, all day, out of a shared inbox. If they're slow, they lose the deal to a competitor.

Heron automates that manual work. We read the emails, classify the documents, extract and enrich the data, run evaluation rules, and push decision-ready output straight into the customer's CRM - in minutes, not hours. 150+ funders, brokers, fintechs and insurers use us in production today. We're growing fast, with customers pulling us into new problems and verticals faster than we can staff them.

We're hiring a Product Manager to take ownership of one of our core verticals. You'll talk to customers, define what to build, ship it with engineering, and measure whether it's working. You'll have real influence over the roadmap and the autonomy to make calls. This is a hands-on IC role - no layers between you and the problem.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own a product vertical end-to-end: Run discovery, write specs, prioritize the backlog, and work with engineering to ship improvements that customers love

 - Be the voice of the customer: Build deep relationships with customers in your vertical, run regular feedback sessions, and translate their pain points into product opportunities

 - Ship and iterate: Move fast, measure impact, and iterate. We care about outcomes, not output - a shipped feature that moves a metric beats a perfect spec that sits in a backlog

 - Set product strategy: Build and present a clear product strategy grounded in customer data, competitive insight, and business impact

 - Collaborate across the company: Work closely with engineering, deployment, sales, and customer success to make sure what we build lands well


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2-4 years of experience in product management at a B2B SaaS company, or 2-3 years in a commercial generalist role before transitioning into tech or product

 - You've shipped software products and can point to specific outcomes you drove

 - You're comfortable with technical concepts - you can discuss APIs, data pipelines, and system architecture with engineers

 - You're a structured thinker who can take ambiguous problems and break them into clear, actionable product decisions

 - You have strong customer instincts, and you'd generally rather spend an hour with a customer than an hour in a planning meeting

Above all else, we value people who want to make an impact in their work. Here are some of the behaviors that we expect of all Herons:

 - Scrappy and creative: when faced with an obstacle, you will either overcome it by pure determination (i.e., by hand), or find an unconventional approach or ‘hack’ to navigate it.

 - Persistent: you may not always find the right answer or person immediately, but you're motivated to keep on trying until you do so. You’ll go the extra mile - including Flying to Gainesville - if that’s what it takes.

 - Pilot not passenger: at a fast-growing company like Heron we need people who jump on opportunities unprompted and drive them to a great outcome, rather than folks who wait to be told what to do.

 - Excited to work in a fast-paced, high-growth, team-based environment: we aim to move quickly and decisively, but together. You’ll be a great team player and do what it takes for the team to succeed.


WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT AS A HERON

 - Com

source_jobs.title: Product Engineers (NYC)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking high-performing NYC-based engineers of any level to help us solve more of our customers’ problems. We are looking for folks who are excited about designing elegant technical solutions and shipping great code frequently. If you are looking for a fresh challenge at a fast-growing AI startup - we would love to hear from you!




WHAT YOU’LL DO

This is a hands-on (‘individual contributor’) role focused on building product that will solve our customers’ problems, including specifically:

 - Designing, building and maintaining backend services and APIs

 - Designing and building new UI/web components

 - Developing new integrations to third party services and AI models

 - Help triage bugs, investigate production incidents, and contribute to postmortems and root cause analysis

 - Participating in code reviews, collaborating across technical and non-technical teams and stakeholders


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Our ideal hire will:

 - Enjoy -actually- building and making an impact: you get a kick out of shipping code frequently, iteratively, and collaboratively. You value MVPs and customer impact over perfectly engineered systems. You can take responsibility over the end-to-end deployment of what you build.

 - Thrive at solving tough technical problems: you enjoy breaking down challenges into simple and well-designed solutions. You are fact-oriented and thoughtful in your approach, but you don’t get bogged down in over-intellectualizing or over-complicating things.

 - Scale-oriented: while having a bias to action and impact, you will care about scalable infrastructure and engineering processes. Even better if you’re able to bring some better practices or improvements to our infrastructure or ways of working!

 - Be able to contribute in our stack: we try to balance speed and impact with fitting the best framework/ technology to the task at hand. Currently, we work primarily in Python / Flask / FastAPI on the backend, Terraform / GCP / AWS for infra, and React / JavaScript / TypeScript / Vercel on the frontend. Ideally, you’ll be able to contribute within our stack while also helping us experiment and evolve to continually improve functionality and performance.

Above all else, we value people who want to make an impact in their work. Here are some of the behaviors that we expect of all Herons:

 - Scrappy and creative: when faced with an obstacle, you will either overcome it by pure determination (i.e., by hand), or find an unconventional approach or ‘hack’ to navigate it.

 - Persistent: you may not always find the right answer or person immediately, but you're motivated to keep on trying until you do so. You’ll go the extra mile - including Flying to Gainesville - if that’s what it takes.

 - Pilot not passenger: at a fast-growing company like Heron we need people who jump on opportunities unprompted and drive them to a great outcome, rather than folks who wait to be told what to do.

 - Excited to work in a fast-paced, high-growth, team-based environment: we aim to move quickly and decisively, but together. You’ll be a great team player and do what it takes for the team to succeed.


WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT AS A HERON

 - Competitive compensation, including equity

 - Based full-time in NYC, where the team works in-person

 - Your choice of top-of-the-line equipment to fit out your desk in the office

 - Occasional travel to London for retreats and to work with the team there

 - Competitive benefits: health insurance; 401K; uncapped annual leave; parental leave

 - Free mental health support via Spill - on-demand access to therapy

 - Weekly company lunches (always inclusive and vegan-friendly - especially if you like Korean or Vietnamese food!)

 - Applicants from any background and gender are welcome and we have a zero tolerance policy on discrimination


ABOUT THE INTERVIEW

 - Initial conversation (20 minutes) - conversation to discuss what you're looking for, what Heron offers, and to see if there

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Heron builds document automation for lenders, funders, fintechs and more: we process bank statements, tax returns, and financial applications so that financing decisions happen faster, more accurately, and at scale. We're hiring Platform Engineers to build the systems that make all of this work.




THE PROBLEM

Our platform processes millions of sensitive financial documents for customers. As we onboard more lenders and expand into new verticals, the systems underneath need to get better, not just bigger. That means:

 - Reliability at scale: Our customers handle large volumes of documents and data, and we operate at the core of their business. When they process an application through Heron, it needs to work every time. You'll build the infrastructure that ensures our backend stays fast and reliable as volume grows.

 - Developer experience: We have a ~15-person engineering team shipping features across multiple product lines. You'll build the internal tools, abstractions, and platform primitives that let product engineers move fast without breaking things.

 - Product thinking (yes, even for internal tools): You're building for an internal audience (namely our pre-sales and post-sales teams, and other engineers), but you treat it like a product. That means understanding what we are trying to achieve for customers, designing intuitive tooling that makes that easy to do, and then iterating based on ‘customer’ feedback.

 - Security and compliance: This is critical to our customers — sensitive financial and deal data must be handled to exacting standards. This isn't an afterthought; it's a core platform requirement.

This is the kind of work that compounds. The better the platform, the faster every team at Heron can ship, and the faster we can deliver value to our customers.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

This is a hands-on IC role on our Platform tribe, working directly with our CTO and Head of Engineering, and alongside a strong team of engineers.

In your first few months, you'll:

 - Get into the codebase and start working on the Platform backlog within your first week. We don't believe in dragging out ramp-ups; you'll be reviewing and shipping code quickly.

 - Own the development of meaningful features on either our Product Platform (the building blocks like workflow orchestration & execution, entity management, and workflow tooling) or Platform Infra (scalability, resilience, CI/CD, security).

 - Debug real customer issues end-to-end, from support ticket to production fix, gaining deep understanding of how the system works under load.

 - Contribute to the architectural decisions that shape how Heron's backend evolves as the volume we handle grows.

Longer-term, there's room to grow into a tech lead role as the platform team scales. But first and foremost, we need builders.




WHO THRIVES HERE

Our ideal candidate is a backend engineer with 3-6 years of experience who has worked at a startup or growth-stage company and knows what it feels like to build something that actually matters. You're more drawn to the systems layer than the UI layer, and you get satisfaction from making other engineers more productive.

More specifically:

 - You have a strong CS foundation (degree or equivalent depth) and have a track record of writing clean, well-tested Python in production.

 - You've built backend systems at a startup or growth-stage company and understand the trade-offs between speed and quality at that stage.

 - You're comfortable with cloud infrastructure (we're on GCP), databases, CI/CD, and the unglamorous work that keeps a platform running.

 - You care about reliability and developer experience. You might have fixed a flaky test suite in a previous job or built a simple but killer internal tool that everyone uses.

 - You don't need to be told what to do - pilot not passenger. You see problems, propose solutions, and ship them.

 - You're less interested in being the person on customer calls and more interested in being the p

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representatives (SDRs)
source_jobs.description:
Note: this is a sales role, so if you’re applying, don’t just send us your CV. Get creative and sell us on you!




SALES DEVELOPMENT REPRESENTATIVES

We’re hiring Sales Development Representatives (SDRs) to help us sell Heron into our existing and new industry verticals. The focus of the role will be traditional outbound sales, but also include a range of go-to-market (GTM) activities like marketing and partnerships - whatever it takes to grow revenue. You’ll get to work alongside Heron’s co-founders and other experienced, proven sellers and learn directly from them. This role is an amazing way to break into software sales and progress into an Account Executive or a similar role in the future.

No prior full-time experience required: if you come from a commercial background and want to play a meaningful role in the growth of a fast-moving company, we’d love to hear from you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

This is a hands-on role focused on growing revenue and bringing in new customers. Examples of what you might do:

 - Prospect, qualify, and book demos with new customers via email, LinkedIn, and other outbound channels

 - Support and eventually lead customer demos and trials, from initial setup through to handoff or close

 - Follow up diligently after calls and demos - whether that’s sending recaps, chasing next steps, or coordinating internal follow-ups

 - Track pipeline progress and share learnings with the team to improve our go-to-market approach

 - Experiment with other go-to-market approaches, including paid marketing, partnerships, and anything else that might help us drive pipeline and revenue


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We expect that strong candidates will have the following experience:

 - Current SDR/ BDR or equivalent with 1-2 years in a fast-paced startup or sales organization - now looking to take on more ownership across the GTM motion

 - Entrepreneurial recent graduate - maybe without much formal or full-time experience but demonstrated sales acumen and ability to hustle in other contexts (e.g. student businesses, side projects, internships).

 - Commercial generalist in a startup or professional services/ consulting with some exposure to sales or customer work - now looking to double down on a sales and growth role

Regardless of specific background, our ideal hire will be:

 - Execution-focused: You move fast, follow through, and get things done without a lot of hand-holding

 - Commercially sharp: You understand what makes a customer tick and enjoy the challenge of selling into a new market

 - Clear communicator: You’re direct, thoughtful, and good at tailoring your message to your audience

 - Curious and coachable: You’re hungry to learn, ask good questions, and improve every week

Above all else, we value people who want to make an impact in their work. Here are some of the behaviors that we expect of all Herons:

 - Scrappy and creative: when faced with an obstacle, you will either overcome it by pure determination (i.e., by hand), or find an unconventional approach or ‘hack’ to navigate it.

 - Persistent: you may not always find the right answer or person immediately, but you're motivated to keep on trying until you do so. You’ll go the extra mile - including Flying to Gainesville - if that’s what it takes.

 - Pilot not passenger: at a fast-growing company like Heron we need people who jump on opportunities unprompted and drive them to a great outcome, rather than folks who wait to be told what to do.

 - Excited to work in a fast-paced, high-growth, team-based environment: we aim to move quickly and decisively, but together. You’ll be a great team player and do what it takes for the team to succeed.


WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT AS A HERON

 - Competitive compensation, including equity and uncapped commission. Please note that advertised salary is the expected OTE of base + commission, but we do not cap commission and you’ll earn commission on every deal you work on

 - Based full-time, and in-person in our NYC

source_jobs.title: Principal Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Heron processes millions of sensitive financial documents for some of the fastest-growing MCA Funders, Brokers, Insurers, and Fintechs in the US and UK. source_jobs.title: Finance Associate
source_jobs.description:
MISSION OF THE ROLE

We're hiring a Finance Associate to own the operational, in-the-weeds finance work that keeps Heron running — accounts receivable and billing (one of our most important and trust-critical customer touchpoints), burn reconciliation, revenue reconciliation, and the numbers that feed our investor reporting and business reviews. As we scale, we want someone who can take full ownership of these workflows and run them reliably and independently — becoming the person who owns how operational finance actually runs day to day.




THE OPPORTUNITY

Finance and BizOps are a central enabler at Heron. The function isn't a back office — it sits at the intersection of every team, and deep financial knowledge of the business is what lets us connect dots that aren't obvious from any single seat.

You'll be hands-on and exacting from day one: owning accounts receivable, getting billing right, reconciling burn and revenue. This is a finance-heavy, operational role — the work is in the details, and owning it well is the job. Over time, there's real room to grow: more scope as the company scales, ownership of how the whole operational finance function runs, and a front-row seat to the financial story we tell investors as we raise. You'll be in the finance mix of a fast-growing startup, with the chance to build something you own.

This is a role for someone who genuinely enjoys getting the details right and wants to own operational finance at a scaling startup — running finance operations exceptionally well, while also being a key partner on the forward-looking side (planning, budgeting, and pressure-testing the model).




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own accounts receivable end-to-end — billing is one of our most important and direct customer touchpoints, and getting it right protects both revenue and customer trust. You'll work cross-functionally to make sure every customer is billed correctly, run the month-end billing process, and own the AR function as it scales

 - Run month-end finance cycles — own burn calculation and reconciliation across our banking and spend stack, plus the data inputs that feed business reviews

 - Own revenue reporting investors can trust — reconcile billing and CRM data against our source of truth, untangle the messy edge cases, and build out the retention and revenue metrics (e.g. GRR, NRR, cohort views by segment) that show how the business is really performing and that go to our board and investors

 - Contextualize the numbers — understand where we're spending and investing as a company, and what the numbers mean in the broader context of the business

 - Support planning & FP&A — partner on our financial model, budgeting, and forecasting: pull together the inputs, pressure-test assumptions, and help translate the numbers into a forward view.

 - Automate the finance function — find the repetitive, manual work and build it away; help move us toward a finance function that scales without linearly adding headcount

 - Use AI deeply — you'll lean on AI heavily, both to automate finance work and to dig into how our product actually generates revenue, so the numbers always reflect what's really happening under the hood


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Our ideal hire has an accounting or finance background (2–5 years of experience) and genuinely enjoys the detail-oriented work of getting the numbers exactly right, even when it's painstaking. The best fit is someone who wants to own operational finance at a startup — either an accountant ready to move out of traditional accounting into something more impactful, or someone who's already worked in the finance function of a startup and wants more ownership of how it runs. What matters most: you're excited to run finance operations exceptionally well, not impatient to skip past them.

Regardless of background, you'll be:

 - Detail-obsessed where it counts — you take real satisfaction in getting the numbers exactly right, and you don't drop the ball

 - A collaborative

source_jobs.title: Deployment Specialists
source_jobs.description:
DEPLOYMENT SPECIALISTS

We are seeking high-output, technically-minded problem solvers to lead customer trials and deployments. You’ll be on the front lines of onboarding customers and ensuring they have a smooth go-live experience. If you're dependable, detail-oriented, and thrive in fast-paced environments, we’d love to hear from you!




THE OPPORTUNITY

From day one, you’ll be hands-on: supporting new customer onboarding, resolving live issues, and collaborating closely with engineering to improve our platform.

You’ll become a key member of our small NYC team, working directly with founders, engineers, and customers. If a process is broken, unclear, or missing entirely - you’ll help fix it.

As we grow, there will be opportunities to take on broader responsibilities across product, customer success, and internal operations. If you’re someone who thrives in a fast-moving environment and loves making things work better - especially in a customer-facing context - this is the role for you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

This is a hands-on, customer-facing role focused on running and improving core processes. Examples of what you might do:

 - Run successful trials: manage and run trials for prospects, including defining success criteria, managing the trial process so those criteria are met and converting trials to paying customers.

 - Deploy new customers: coordinate and implement deployments, including defining and detailing customer requirements, configuring our product to meet those needs, then testing and validating as required to get our customers live.

 - Improve our operations: help us run a tighter ship by refining our tools and internal workflows.

 - Shape the product: surface recurring issues or feature gaps to inform what we build.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We expect that strong candidates will have the following experience:

 - Operations generalists with technical and/ or customer-facing experience in startups or professional services/ consulting - you’ve built a strong skillset and now want to dive into deploying software for customers

 - Support engineers or technical customer operations professionals - you are used to fixing customers’ biggest and most urgent issues and this is your opportunity to take even more ownership of customer relationships and a product

 - Technical product managers who are now looking to move into a more hands-on and technical role, where you’ll be working directly with users and our product - not just steering things behind the scenes

Regardless of specific background, our ideal hire will be:

 - Highly productive: You get a lot done and thrive in fast-paced, high-output environments. You take pride in making problems disappear.

 - Technically inclined: You’re not an engineer, but you’re confident working with data, understanding technical systems, and digging into details.

 - A natural problem-solver: You’re resourceful and persistent. When something’s broken, you roll up your sleeves and figure it out.

 - Detail-oriented and dependable: You catch issues others might miss and can be trusted to follow through with minimal oversight.

 - A clear communicator: You explain things clearly and concisely, whether to a customer or a teammate.

Above all else, we value people who want to make an impact in their work. Here are some of the behaviors that we expect of all Herons:

 - Scrappy and creative: when faced with an obstacle, you will either overcome it by pure determination (i.e., by hand), or find an unconventional approach or ‘hack’ to navigate it.

 - Persistent: you may not always find the right answer or person immediately, but you're motivated to keep on trying until you do so. You’ll go the extra mile - including Flying to Gainesville - if that’s what it takes.

 - Pilot not passenger: at a fast-growing company like Heron we need people who jump on opportunities unprompted and drive them to a great outcome, rather than folks who wait to be told what to do.

 - Excited to work in a

source_jobs.title: Product Engineers (London)

(Applicants must be based in London, UK)

We are seeking high-performing London-based engineers of any level to help us solve more of our customers’ problems.
